The 42nd Annual St. Petersburg Power & Sailboat Show takes place Thursday, Dec. 5 through Sunday, Dec. 8, 2019 at the Duke Energy Center for the Arts, Mahaffey Theater Yacht Basin and Albert Whitted Park in St. Petersburg, Florida.

Come aboard and explore an extraordinary selection of power boats and sailboats in water and on land. Catch up with boating experts and learn about the latest features and innovations in the marine industry. Show-goers will be excited to find hundreds of power boats and sailboats including family cruisers, runabouts, fishing boats, magnificent sailing yachts, personal watercraft and so much more!

The 47th Annual Fort Myers Boat Show once again takes over the historic downtown riverfront November 21-24.


From Centennial Park to the Fort Myers City Yacht Basin, boats of all styles and sizes from over 130 manufacturers will be displayed on land and in the water. Hundreds of accessory exhibits feature everything from engines and electronics to boat lifts, boating services, fishing gear, clothing and just about anything else you might need on the water. Coastal Angler Magazine fishing seminars will feature presentations by local guides and experts throughout the day. Many boating organizations will also be providing boating safety and how-to information.


Admission is just $13 and children under 16 are free with a paid adult. Tickets may be purchased at the main show entrance (west side of Centennial Park) or the east entrance (Lee St & Edwards Dr). Take advantage of convenient remote parking at First St & Altamont Ave, just west of the show entrance. Parking is only $5 and includes a free two-minute shuttle ride that brings you right to the show entrance.


Not only is this the most complete display of boats and boating products on Florida’s West Coast, it is also one of the most beautiful boat show settings in the country. Plan to spend the day enjoying the show and downtown Fort Myers, where you’ll find a variety of popular restaurants and bars just a block away from the show.


Location: Centennial Park

2000 W First St

Fort Myers, FL


Hours:

Thursday, November 21: 10am-6pm

Friday, November 22: 10am-6pm

Saturday, November 23: 10am-6pm

Sunday, November 24: 10am-5pm
